title: Build the Knowledge Wiki at oma setup and use Graphify as the runtime query substrate via its MCP surface
context: OMA frames reliability as a dual-axis: Ontology (correctness) validates artifacts AFTER generation via oma validate, and Harness (safety) denies tool calls BEFORE they run. Neither tells an agent, before it authors a typed entity, what the domain already knows — existing enums, prior ADR rationale, naming conventions. This is the root of the canonical 'deployment target' drift (one skill meant a cluster name, another the eks|ec2|lambda enum); validation only caught the clash after both documents diverged. docs/docs/knowledge-wiki.md proposes a pre-generation retrieval layer to close this, with three open questions (source-of-truth direction, staleness, retrieval surface). Separately, the marketplace delivery gap (#60) means anything living outside a plugin root is not shipped by /plugin install. Graphify (graphify.net, MIT) was evaluated as a candidate: a multi-modal knowledge-graph builder (Tree-sitter AST + LLM semantic extraction -> NetworkX graph, Leiden clustering, provenance-tagged EXTRACTED/INFERRED/AMBIGUOUS edges, rationale_for edges) whose DEFINING outputs are markdown — GRAPH_REPORT.md always, plus a --wiki mode emitting Wikipedia-style markdown per community — backed by a queryable graph.json. Graphify also exposes a --mcp server and an intrusive 'graphify claude install' that writes its own CLAUDE.md + PreToolUse hook; the latter would collide with OMA's harness, which already owns the PreToolUse surface. The setup-time intent captured from the maintainer: at oma setup, FIRST generate the LLM wiki corpus, THEN run Graphify over it, and from then on answer subsequent agent queries AGAINST Graphify as the runtime query substrate. The corpus is seeded from the ontology schemas plus ADR/Spec plus docs/ in full AND the user's live .omao/ontology instances (Budget/Incident/Deployment), so it indexes the living ontology rather than only the static schemas.
decision: Build the wiki at oma setup time on the user's machine and use Graphify as the RUNTIME query substrate, accessed only through its MCP surface. Setup sequence: (step 1) generate the LLM wiki corpus from the curated source set — schemas/ontology/*.schema.json, ADR/Spec entities, docs/ in full, and the user's live .omao/ontology instances; (step 2) run Graphify over that corpus to build graph.json + the markdown wiki, with extraction strictly one-way (corpus -> graph; never written back), which answers the source-of-truth question by construction; (step 3) from then on, agents answer ontology/codebase queries AGAINST Graphify rather than re-reading raw schemas/docs. (1) Retrieval surface is Graphify's --mcp stdio server, registered through OMA's existing .mcp.json mechanism, NOT 'graphify claude install'. The intrusive installer (which writes its own CLAUDE.md + PreToolUse hook) is never invoked, so OMA's harness PreToolUse hook stays the sole owner of that surface; the harness (matcher '.*') instead WRAPS the agent's Graphify MCP calls. (2) The 'query the wiki before authoring an entity' nudge is delivered via OMA's own steering / CLAUDE.md directives (which OMA already owns), not via Graphify's hook. (3) Indexing the live .omao/ontology instances — not just static schemas — is deliberate: it makes the wiki reflect the LIVING ontology (the Outer Loop's operational feedback), which a frozen schema-only corpus cannot. (4) Staleness is guarded by re-running Graphify incrementally (graphify --update / --watch, or a git post-commit hook) on ontology/docs changes; a frozen index lagging the schemas is worse than none. (5) Graceful degradation is REQUIRED: when graphifyy is not installed or the MCP server is down, agents fall back to reading raw schemas/docs directly — the wiki is an accelerator, never a hard precondition for correctness. (6) The human touch-point is intent only, asked once at oma setup (corpus scope + purpose); the agent then builds the corpus and authors the resulting ADR entity itself — ADRs are ontology entities OMA produces (adr.schema.json), not human-reviewed approval gates.
consequences: Positive: the Knowledge Wiki (#54) becomes implementable with Graphify as a concrete query substrate; building over docs/ PLUS the live .omao/ontology instances is large and repeatedly queried, which is exactly the build-once-query-many regime where Graphify's reported ~71.5x token reduction (1.7k vs 123k tokens/query on a mixed corpus) actually holds — so the efficiency gain is real here, unlike a schema-only corpus that already fits in a prompt; indexing live instances reflects the LIVING ontology, aligning with the Outer Loop; using --mcp keeps the harness as sole PreToolUse owner; pre-query grounding still prevents drift at authoring time. Negative / to verify during implementation: (a) this introduces a RUNTIME dependency — pip install graphifyy, a model API for the semantic pass, and a running MCP process — reversing the earlier build-time-static plan; the #60 goal of shipping a zero-runtime-dependency corpus via /plugin install is GIVEN UP, so the plugin instead ships the wiring (a .mcp.json entry + steering + the oma setup step) and the corpus is built locally; (b) graceful degradation to raw schema/doc reads is mandatory for when graphifyy is absent or the MCP server is down — the wiki must never be a hard precondition for correctness; (c) Graphify cannot run in the current dev environment (no pip), so the setup build is unverified here — this ADR records the decision and the corpus shape (graph.json), not a graphify-produced build; (d) the ~71.5x figure is the vendor's self-reported number, never reproduced in OMA — it must be measured on the real corpus before being claimed in docs; (e) Leiden clustering is tuned for large repos; on the curated ontology slice it may collapse or over-split (fallback: --cluster-only retune or skip clustering); (f) a re-index trigger (graphify --update/--watch or a git hook) is required or the index silently lags the live ontology, which is worse than none; (g) the single-maintainer dependency now sits on the runtime hot path, not just generation — a vendoring/pinning story is needed before GA. Neutral: Graphify's separate value as an opt-in whole-repo comprehension aid for modernization/aidlc is tracked independently in #64 and is out of scope for this ADR.
